column1: ## - hotspots: comma-separated AA changes (HGVSp short) considered DRIVER when seen as missense
## - loss_of_function_target: 1 if any truncating/frameshift/stop_gained should be classified DRIVER
## - role: oncogene | tsg | tf | enzyme | epigenetic | metabolic | fusion_partner
## Source: hand-curated v0.1 by Mark Zobeck from the v2 COG proposal target set in conf/targets.yaml.
## Hotspot residues drawn from the literature cited in plans/pilot_ccdi_mci/rms_translational_pilot_project.md case studies and standard RMS reviews (Shern 2014/2021, Crose 2012, Olanich 2015).

column6: notes
Mut creates WEE1/CHK1 synthetic-lethal dependency.
RAS family hotspot mutations activate MAPK.
G12C-specific inhibitors apply only to G12C.
HRAS-selective tipifarnib applicable.
Neomorphic; cooperates with PIK3CA; defines a high-risk FN-RMS subtype.
Kinase-domain activating mutations in ~10% RMS (Shern 2014).
9p21 homozygous loss in 15-20% FN-RMS; creates CDK4/6 dependence; co-deletes MTAP.
LoF activates RAS-MAPK; selumetinib FDA-approved for pediatric NF1.
12q13-14 amplification in ~10% FP-RMS; CDK4/6 inhibitors directly applicable.
Compensatory partner of CDK4.
Co-deleted with CDKN2A at 9p21; loss creates PRMT5 synthetic lethality.
Targetable when amplified in TP53-wildtype tumors.
Co-occupies 95% of PAX3-FOXO1 super-enhancers.
BAF-complex member; potential FP-RMS dependency.
NuRD complex at 90% of fusion-bound super-enhancers; no clinical inhibitor.
Synthetic lethal in MTAP-null tumors.
PAX3-FOXO1 fusion driver in FP-RMS; targeted indirectly via super-enhancer disruption.
PAX7-FOXO1 fusion driver in a subset of FP-RMS.
Fusion partner in FP-RMS; not directly druggable.
Loss potentiates RAS signaling; PI3K + MEK combination rationale.
Activating mutations in FN-RMS subsets.
